_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Develop detailed operational and tactical plans and orders supporting cyberspace operations requirements at the tactical levels of warfare.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of model deployment strategies, including batch inference, real-time serving, edge deployment, A/B testing, canary releases, and shadow deployments, and the infrastructure requirements and failure modes of each.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-023 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-T-007 (DG, cosine: 0.434), DG-S-009 (DG, cosine: 0.399).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.2/10 (raw 0.325). Shared vocabulary: [requirements]. DCWF-only terms [cyberspace, detailed, develop, levels, operational, operations] vs WIDAI-only terms [b, batch, canary, deployment, deployments, edge] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.846 (P=0.863, R=0.829). P exceeds R by 0.034 — the DCWF element's 18 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 28 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Develop detailed operational and tactical plans and orders supporting cyberspace" and "Knowledge of model deployment strategies, including batch inference, real-time s" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.444 > STS 0.325 (gap +0.119). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [requirements] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.5%. Shared tokens: [requirements]. DCWF-only: [cyberspace, detailed, develop, levels, operational, operations]. WIDAI-only: [b, batch, canary, deployment, deployments, edge].
